[Adblock Plus 2.0]
! Title: WebSpace test EasyList sample (curated)
! Hand-authored representative subset for end-to-end parser→shim
! testing. Mirrors real EasyList rule shapes; one of each
! pseudo-class / modifier we want to assert against.
!
! Stability contract: this file is the input to the
! `easylist_sample_*.js` fixtures emitted by tool/dump_shim_js.dart
! and exercised by test/js/content_blocker_easylist_sample.test.js.
! Adding/removing rules requires re-running the dumper and updating
! the test's expectations.

! ---- Network blocks (||domain^) ----
||doubleclick.net^
||googlesyndication.com^
||google-analytics.com^
||tracker.example^

! ---- Network exceptions (@@||domain^) ----
@@||cdn.googlesyndication.com^

! ---- Generic cosmetic ----
##.advert
##.ad-banner
##.sponsored-content
##div[data-ad-slot]
##a[href*="doubleclick.net"]

! ---- Standard CSS :has() (passes through unchanged) ----
##div.article:has(div.adsbygoogle)

! ---- ABP :-abp-has() (rewritten to :has) ----
##div.feed-item:-abp-has(.ad-tag)

! ---- :has-text() (converts to text rule) ----
##span.notice:has-text(Sponsored)

! ---- :contains() (converts to text rule) ----
##p.story-body:contains(Advertisement)

! ---- Domain-specific cosmetic ----
linkedin.com##.feed-shared-update-v2--ad
cnn.com##div[data-zone-id="ad"]

! ---- Domain-specific :-abp-has ----
linkedin.com##div.feed-shared-update-v2:-abp-has(.feed-shared-actor__sub-description)

! ---- Domain-specific text rule (#?# with :-abp-contains) ----
linkedin.com#?#div.feed-shared-actor:-abp-contains(/Promoted|Sponsored/)

! ---- Multi-domain cosmetic ----
example.com,test.com##.cross-site-ad

! ---- Path-anchored network rules (||domain/path, ||domain^*x) ----
! Parser-only fixtures — the Dart-side isBlocked tests assert these
! match correct URL paths. Not wired into abp_rule_probe.html's
! network section because the verdict would split by platform: on
! iOS/macOS the JS-bridge `blockCheck` calls Dart `isBlocked(url)`
! which sees the path and fires; on Android the native
! FastSubresourceInterceptor only sees host, so a sub-resource hit
! won't fire (main-document navigation still does, since
! `shouldOverrideUrlLoading` calls isBlocked with the full URL). A
! probe row would need platform-specific "expected" labels to avoid
! looking like a bug on Android — out of scope here.
||pathblock.example/ads/
||pathblock.example^/track
||pathblock.example^*pixel.gif

! ---- uBO :style() cosmetic extension ----
! Namespaced under fp_probe_* so production lists won't collide with
! the targets in test/fixtures/abp_rule_probe.html — same convention
! the cosmetic FP guards use.
##.fp_probe_styled_banner:style(height: 1px !important)
linkedin.com##.fp_probe_styled_promo:style(opacity: 0.1 !important)

! ---- Skipped rule shapes (must not break parsing) ----
! These are intentional gaps in the Dart parser. The Rust engine
! (`useRustAdblockEngine` opt-in) DOES handle the redirect / csp /
! removeparam shapes below; the Dart parser skips them.
##^script[src*="ads.js"]
##.foo:matches-path(/section/)
##.bar:min-text-length(20)
/banner-\d+\.gif/
#$#log "snippet"

! ---- Rust-engine-only rules ----
! Handled by adblock-rust when the Rust engine flag is on; ignored by
! the Dart parser. Probe targets are namespaced `fp_probe_*` to keep
! production list collisions away from the abp_rule_probe.html FP
! rows.
||fp.probe.removeparam.example$removeparam=utm_source
||fp.probe.removeparam.example$removeparam=fbclid
||fp.probe.csp.example$csp=script-src 'none'

! ---- Procedural cosmetic (uBO action operators) ----
! :remove(), :upward(), :style(), :remove-attr(), :remove-class().
! Resolved by the Rust engine; rendered via
! procedural_cosmetic_shim.dart at DOCUMENT_END.
!
! IMPORTANT: adblock-rust silently DROPS global procedural rules at
! parse time (cosmetic_filter_cache_builder.rs:106 — "Procedural
! cosmetic filters cannot be generic"). They MUST be hostname-
! scoped or the engine ignores them entirely. Scoping to `localhost`
! makes the abp_rule_probe.html section fire when the user serves
! the probe via `python3 -m http.server` — the canonical way to
! test the feature, since file:/// has no hostname for the engine
! to scope against either. Production filter lists (EasyList,
! AdGuard, …) ship these rules already hostname-scoped to real
! domains; this section mirrors their shape.
localhost##div.fp_probe_proc_remove:has-text(REMOVE-ME):remove()
localhost##div.fp_probe_proc_upward:has-text(LEAF):upward(1):remove()
localhost##div.fp_probe_proc_style:has-text(Sponsored):style(outline: 2px solid red !important)
localhost##div.fp_probe_proc_remove_attr[data-tracker]:remove-attr(data-tracker)
localhost##div.fp_probe_proc_remove_class.fp_probe_remove_me:remove-class(fp_probe_remove_me)

! ---- Synthetic rules used by test/fixtures/abp_rule_probe.html ----
! False-positive guards in the probe page rely on these — selectors
! crafted to be unique enough that no production filter list (EasyList,
! EasyPrivacy, etc.) will also target them, so the FP rows give a clean
! signal regardless of which other lists the user has enabled.
##.fp_probe_class
##div[fp-probe-attr]
##a[href*="fp.probe.example"]
##span.fp_probe_notice:has-text(FpProbeMagicNeedle)
##div.fp_probe_article:has(div.fp_probe_descendant)
##div.fp_probe_feed:-abp-has(.fp_probe_tag)
